item.title: My Catwoman Cosplay
item.description:
SnarkyJayCosplay here with my new Catwoman cosplay. This one’s inspired mostly by Clay Mann and Joelle Jones (for the photos without the cowl).
I thought of the Batmilk bottle myself and it was such a huge hit at Megacon! Special shout out to my friend jcurtis_atelier, who made this cowl for me. So talented.

item.title: New York removes last of its kind historical artifact
item.description: New York City's LAST public pay-phone booth, at 50th and 7th has been removed.
item.dc:creator: Igotfourbedrats

item.title: I was stuck in an abusive relationship for three years. This is all that’s left of my ex.
item.description: I was physically and emotionally abused for three years, trapped by my visual impairment making it difficult to transport myself. Repeated failure of first responders and mandatory reporters to help or provide resources was used against me. Cops finally arrested my ex in March, giving me a 72 hour window of safety and resources that could help in the meantime. I was granted a temporary restraining order which was violated. I was granted a four year restraining order on the first of April. It has also been violated. I had to quit my job due to fear at home, and concerns for my safety. My ex has repeatedly tried to file civil cases against me. I had to get a victims rights lawyer. A judge has thrown them all out. There’s no anonymous address I can go to- all shelters and programs are full (that accept men). I was financially abused and left with a trashed house, a ton of debt, and unpaid utility bills. There are no programs in my state that help domestic violence victims without children with rent or utilities. Because I went on leave with my job before quitting, I can’t get unemployment. I literally lost everything. But. My ex never dealt with his stuff. I’d been cleaning and packing and realized I really didn’t have anything material. I had almost no physical possessions. What I did have, was a roof over my head for the time being, a dog, a therapist, a landlord being understanding, and my future ahead of me for the first time in a long time. I was almost done with my undergraduate in math, so I went back to school at time and a half so I can graduate in December. I focused on creating social connections locally, something that had been impossible previously. I hike with my dog every day. Behind me in this picture is the last of the physical belongings I need to sort through. My house is almost mine again. I don’t know what I’m feeling right now. I still don’t feel safe - but I feel like soon, I can have something that’s mine. Time to finish up.
item.dc:creator: deilanntau

item.title: Imgurian cookbook? Let's make homemade water
item.description: I was so excited to see Imgur dedicate a day to cooking and recipes! If you've seen my posts, you know I love to cook. I wanted to come up with a recipe I haven't shared before. This is my family's homemade water recipe (with a few tweaks I've made over the years). It's pretty simple and forgiving if you miss a step. For example the flour pinch isn't necessary but you might want to strain it through a french press if you skip it. I did this off camera because I ran out of time. Anyways, enjoy! Ingredients: 3 ice cubes Water 1/4 cup or 59ml of water Recipe: Start by adding 2 ice cubes to a mortar and pestle and grinding it up into a slushy. Bring a sauce pan to high heat and add in your slushy water. Once it heats up you're going to get a water consistency. Cut the heat and let it cool down. Finely chop up 1 ice cube. Add your ice cube into a blender and pulse for 30 seconds. Then take the water you sauteed earlier and add it to the blender. Take that mixture and add it to a cocktail shaker. Shaken not stirred. It's going to be cold so I always wrap a towel around it. Now this next step is optional but I like to add a pinch of flour. It just gives it that je ne sais quoi. Off camera I put it through a french press, unfortunately it didn't fit into the video timeframe. Once it's strained pour it into a bowl. I tried to mix it by hand but my dainty little girl arms couldn't handle it so I had to bust out my KitchenAid. Mix on low for 2-3 minutes. Next you're going to take ¼ cup or 59ml of that water and sprinkle it onto your cutting board. Careful not to spill! Take a rolling pin and roll it until it's about 0.0001mm thin. Then using a pipette suck up the water and pour into a glass of your choice. You know the next part. Enjoy! Let me know if you have any questions about measurements and what not. How do you like to make water?

item.title: I had a Dad win
item.description: My son is way into obscure podcasts. Really wanted to see "welcome to Nightvale" live, but none of his friends are into it so over the last 9 months I listened to every episode. He's so excited and it's wonderful sharing all the inside jokes / obscure references with him. Feels like a big Dad win and I wanted to share!

item.title: Wait for it.....
item.description: The right, with their love of cancel culture, is sure to be calling for the obliteration of "The Simpsons" after last night's episode, "Poorhouse Rock", dropped a nuclear sized bomb on right wing politics. It was beautiful and scary.

item.title: Wrapping up another run of custom builds. Thought yall might want to check out some of my dirty pictures?
item.description:
Ahhh this guy right here is exactly what I look for. Disgusting! I LOVE IT. I'm saving the pretty pictures for the end so try and not peak ahead.
Electro Voice Mercury series. This is a 611 but the 911's looked identical. The 611 is omnidirectional...Was omnidirectional : (
Sad to see in this condition. Strange how we interpret our preference by comparing what IS to what we THINK something should be. I understand some people get upset when they see my end result. I think because it's not what they THINK it should be? I get so excited when I find them like this, the key is try not to spend too much money... lets see what we can do about cleaning him up.
Circa 1950s. 70 + years old, this guy looks like he may have been down on his luck for quite a while.
Gut check. Hmmm. Doesn't look promising for a warranty repair? That's OK. I'm going a slightly different direction anyhow.
Yeah. Pretty gross. The key is to use a ton of chemicals. And punk rock. If I recall correctly there was A LOT of the Cramps, 'Off the Bone'. Freakin' great album if your into a Halloween surf vibe. Oh, and some ventilation.
A lot of this calcium and mineral corrosion is coming off pretty good. Some damage...Something caustic, maybe cat pee? Like really old, it doesn't smell...too much.
Pitting ended up only on this one side that looks like it sat submerged, I clean and fill pits with an epoxy to get a uniform surface to begin a fine surface polish. Lots of nicotine! and it DOES smell. oof. Pro tip: GLOVES.
Outside, ready for finish sanding.
Inside, yeah this is a good time. Now for a little tiny wire wheel. My favorite is the way is shoots little tiny steel wires out at 5,000 rpm. Pro tip #2: Full Face shield!
Purist avert your eyes! I'm not trying to refinish the chrome as much as just give it a sort of uniform brushed finish. Knock off some of the boogers on the edges you know. I want these guys to shine but also show their age. It's a celebration after all.
Polishin' time! This is quality headphone time right here.
Alrighty then. Happy Birthday! We've got ourselves a proper carcass.
So short of re-chroming which is not what I personally am looking to do, what I end up with is the shell of a vintage microphone clean and polished ready rejoin the party in a bit of a different way...
I rebuild these retro shells into decorative accent lamps for home and studio use. These are all salvaged from non working units I obsessively and relentlessly track and buy.
Lots of the units I purchase have missing, cross-threaded, and damaged parts or broken components so sometimes the challenge is to come up with a missing piece or a way to get it to go back together in its original form. Fabrication starts with lots of aluminum extrusion and epoxy. I build a small microprocessor into an LED array suitable for whatever model I find as a good candidate.
I add an optical diffusion in place of the original windscreen to complete the look.
The LED arrays have pixel by pixel animations and 12 color remote controls. The microphone switches have been replaced with new functional power switches that cuts and restores power at the last used setting.
Let me know what you think, I know its not for everyone but I enjoy it so I'm gunna keep rockin them out.
Some folks are really turned off by the color options but you can always just turn them off and use them for display. They don't stink anymore!
I'm working on some matching sets of full size boom stands and desktop models.
I started making these lamps for my studio, I use all RED as an 'ON AIR' signal that I'm recording.
Some of the newly completed full size tripod boom and straight stands. L-R: 1940s Turner 33X. 1950s Shure 55s. 1950s Electrovoice 911. 1950s Shure Unidyne 55s
A look in all Red, makes for a pretty awesome studio vibe, does anyone know Rob Zombie? I feel like me might like these?
Well I hope you liked it. I super appreciate you looking and if you'd like to know more I'll include my instagram link below.
Cat Tax: Do you have change for 2 dog? I take a break from soldering every day to get Mason and Nugget out into the sunshine. It makes Mason sneeze! Thank you SO much for looking. I'm a real dude making handmade crafts here in Anaheim California. I have spent 30 years in pro audio stage lighting and am trying to figure out a new path after Covid has shut down my stage production business and severely limited my physical abilities. If you'd like to know more please check out my Instagram to find my Etsy craft store. https://www.instagram.com/battlestationlighting/ Please share if you know someone that may be looking for the perfect Fathers Day or Graduation present! Feel free to follow and share with your crazy aunt on Facebook! I've received some great feedback here on Imgur and appreciate your support and encouragement, Thank you!
item.dc:creator: BattleStationLighting

item.title: Supreme Court guts rights to Effective Defense protections at trials
item.description: “The decision today is a little-known habeas decision, Shinn v. Martinez Ramirez involving two men in Arizona who have been condemned to death row. The consequences of Martinez Ramirez will also be disastrous for anyone relying on their constitutional right to effective counsel. And, like the Dobbs leak, today’s decision also makes clear that the court’s conservative supermajority is hell bent on smashing and grabbing precedent and constitutional rights no matter the consequences. Shinn v. Martinez Ramirez involves a pair of consolidated cases in which two people who were convicted in Arizona state courts argued that they had received ineffective assistance at their trials. The two defendants in Martinez Ramirez argued that the process of obtaining their convictions or sentences violated the 6th Amendment to the Constitution, which guarantees the effective assistance of counsel. One of the defendants, Barry Jones, maintained his lawyers were so ineffective, they failed to uncover evidence that he was innocent of the crimes. Jones was convicted at trial and sentenced to death. The other defendant argued that his lawyers failed to uncover mitigating evidence that would have persuaded the jury to sentence him to a term of years in prison, rather than the death penalty. That two Arizona defendants would have received constitutionally ineffective assistance at their trials is no accident. Indigent defense – defense for people who lack the resources to hire their own lawyer – is in crisis in this country. Indigent defense is woefully underfunded; and public defenders handle hundreds of cases per year, many more than they have the time or resources to manage effectively. States also heavily restrict the procedures and resources that would allow public defenders to develop their cases in greater depth. Unfortunately, the problem does not end there. Instead, it continues throughout state criminal proceedings into post-conviction proceedings, where defendants are supposed to be able to enforce their 6th Amendment right to effective assistance of counsel. That is because when a defendant receives ineffective assistance of counsel at trial, the trial lawyer can’t simultaneously argue that they are providing ineffective assistance. Neither can the defendant’s lawyer on appeal, when the case is restricted to the record at trial. Ineffective assistance claims often depend on evidence outside the record, like what a lawyer failed to uncover. And so ineffective-assistance-of-trial-counsel claims are raised during post-conviction proceedings that happen after an appeal. During post-conviction proceedings, a defendant is supposed to be able to expand the initial trial record and point to all of the things that his trial lawyer failed to uncover. That’s how it’s supposed to work. But just as there is an indigent defense crisis in this country, there is also a post-conviction crisis. Post-conviction proceedings are woefully underfunded; and lawyers are limited in the time and resources they have to pursue post-conviction relief. So defendants who are represented by ineffective lawyers at trial may then be represented by an ineffective lawyer during their post-conviction proceedings, when they are supposed to be arguing that their trial lawyer was ineffective. And – surprise – the ineffective post-conviction lawyer may fail to argue that the trial lawyer was ineffective, or may fail to develop any evidence in support of that claim. A decade ago, the Supreme Court responded to this problem with a simple and elegant solution. In a pair of decisions, the court said that if a defendant is represented by an ineffective lawyer during their post-conviction proceedings, and that lawyer fails to argue that the defendant was represented by an ineffective lawyer during the defendant’s trial, a federal court can still hear the defendant’s claim that they received ineffective assistance at trial in violation of the 6th Amendment. That’s because the defendant isn’t at fault for failing to present their ineffective-assistance-of-trial counsel claim when, through no wrongdoing on their part, the state appoints an ineffective lawyer to represent them during post-conviction proceedings, and that ineffective lawyer fails to argue the defendant received shoddy legal help during trial. That’s where the law stood before today when the court took a wrecking ball to those decisions. Martinez Ramirez held that there is nothing a federal court can do when a defendant received ineffective assistance at their trial in violation of the 6th Amendment, and was then appointed an ineffective attorney during post-conviction proceedings who did not present evidence to support the claim that the defendant received ineffective assistance at trial. Specifically, the court held that the federal statute governing post-conviction review, the Anti-Terrorism and Effective Death Penalty Act prohibits the federal court from considering evidence that the ineffective post-conviction lawyer failed to uncover. As it did in the case of Jones, this evidence may indicate that the defendant is innocent of the crime for which he was sentenced to death. The court recognized that Martinez Ramirez nullified the prior decisions that offered defendants a shot at relief, writing that “any such hearing” permitted under those decisions “would serve no purpose,” since a federal court could not consider the evidence in deciding whether the defendant’s rights under the 6th Amendment were violated. As Justice Sotomayor wrote in a dissent for the three Democratic appointees, the court’s decision “makes illusory the protections of the 6th Amendment.” Source: https://slate.com/news-and-politics/2022/05/scotus-constitutional-right-habeas-corpus-prison-death-row.html EDIT: Thanks for the “Mind-Blowing” Accolade!
item.dc:creator: runningmandible
